title: 2026 World Cup news live updates: Injuries, storylines as the tournament kicks off - Yahoo Sports
original_text:
2026 World Cup news live updates: Injuries, storylines as the tournament kicks off
The 2026 World Cup is here!
The tournament kicks off Thursday with Mexico facing South Africa at a sold-out Estadio Azteca in Mexico City, setting in motion the biggest World Cup ever staged with 48 teams vying for the title of world champions.
Advertisement
The United States men’s national team will kick off its tournament on Friday night at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles against Paraguay. On Wednesday, center back Chris Richards, who suffered an ankle injury last month, said he was ready to play after missing the friendlies against Senegal and Germany in the lead up to the World Cup.
Elsewhere, teams are settled into the World Cup base camps. Iran arrived in Tijuana on Sunday morning to begin preparations despite ongoing uncertainty surrounding travel logistics and visa processing. Reigning champions Argentina made easy work in their two World Cup tune-ups with a pair of shutouts against Honduras and Iceland. Lionel Messi’s health was a concern heading into June, but he played the final 20 minutes against Iceland and converted a penalty minutes after being subbed on.
Play 2026 Soccer Pick 'Em with FOX One and make your picks for the world's biggest soccer tournament
If you’re looking for World Cup content, we’ve got you covered with players to watch, team power rankings, predictions, questions facing the USMNT, and so much more.
Advertisement
We'll be following it all as the 2026 World Cup gets underway.
Catch up with all the latest World Cup news right here on Yahoo Sports:
Live152 updates
Thu, June 11, 2026 at 12:15 PM EDT
Jay Busbee
The world's soccer fans are coming to America's beloved NFL stadiums, and they're bringing their drums, their scarves, and — most of all — their chants. Intricate, melodic multi-verse chants, sung in rough full-throated unison loud enough to overwhelm any feeble attempt at "U-S-A!"
And we Americans will cheer for our guys in red, white and blue, and maybe we'll manage a "Ref you suck!" every now and then, but all the while we'll be looking over at the chanting masses from beyond our borders and think, That sure looks like fun.
Chants are the lifeblood of soccer (or football, whichever) fandom, a communal expression of joy, sorrow, exultation, grief and humor — sometimes all at once. Every culture has its chants, but soccer has codified them into a vast, rich foundation of community.
Read more here.
CHICAGO, ILLINOIS - JUNE 6: Members of the American Outlaws watch the action during the international friendly match between United States and Germany at Soldier Field on June 6, 2026 in Chicago, Illinois. (Photo by Michael Miller/ISI Photos/ISI Photos via Getty Images)
(Michael Miller/ISI Photos via Getty Images)
Thu, June 11, 2026 at 12:00 PM EDT
Sean Leahy
Thu, June 11, 2026 at 11:15 AM EDT
Sean Leahy
Thu, June 11, 2026 at 10:15 AM EDT
Sean Leahy
The Golden Ball goes to the best player of the tournament; the Golden Boot goes to the top goalscorers; and the Golden Glove is for the best goalkeeper of the tournament.
In 2022, Lionel Messi won the Golden Ball, Kylian Mbappé the Golden Boot and Emiliano Martínez the Golden Glove.
Check out the Yahoo Sports staff World Cup predictions here.
Thu, June 11, 2026 at 10:08 AM EDT
Sean Leahy
Omar Artan, the 34-year-old referee from Somali who was denied entry to work the World Cup, will take charge of August’s UEFA Super Cup match between Paris Saint-Germain and Aston Villa.
“Omar Artan is an excellent young but already experienced referee, who has proven himself at the highest competition level of the Confederation of African Football,” said UEFA president Aleksander Čeferin. “Football is made to connect people, and UEFA wants to show its respect to Omar and his outstanding officiating skills, which had earned him such a prestigious nomination.”
A U.S. official said this week that Artan, who was Africa’s best male referee in 2025, was not allowed in due to an "association with suspected members of terror organizations.” Artan arrived in Mogadishu on Wednesday and was welcomed by supporters and government officials.
The UEFA Super Cup features the winners of the Champions League (PSG) and Europa League (Aston Villa). This summer's edition will take place at Red Bull Arena in Salzburg, Austria.
MOGADISHU, SOMALIA - JUNE 10: Somali referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan greets fans who show support for him, after he was denied entry into the United States where he had traveled to take part in the World Cup organization and was forced to return to his country, in Mogadishu, Somalia on June 10, 2026. (Photo by Abuukar Mohamed Muhidin/Anadolu via Getty Images)
(Anadolu via Getty Images)
Thu, June 11, 2026 at 9:56 AM EDT
Sean Leahy
The dramatic comeback, capped off by OG Anunoby’s late put back sent Knicks fans into a frenzy, even New York’s own Tyler Adams.
Thu, June 11, 2026 at 9:54 AM EDT
Sean Leahy
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM EDT
The United States men's national team opens its World Cup on Friday against Paraguay in Los Angeles, and I'll come right out and say it: There's no shame if you haven't given this team an ounce of your attention since the last World Cup, which ended with a 3-1 loss to the Netherlands in the Round of 16.
New (since the last World Cup) manager Mauricio Pochettino is the most acclaimed coach to ever helm the squad, and he has a deeper and more talented roster than any in USMNT history. But that doesn't mean the USA, despite the historical boost a host nation gets, will go further than its usual ceiling of the final 16 teams. If it does, though, it will be because Pochettino coaxed a new level of quality from the same core of players that anchored the 2022 squad. Surely you remember these players! But in case you don't, here's a primer on who they are and what they can do.
Read more here.
The US national team poses for a photo ahead of the international friendly football match between USA and Germany at Soldier Field in Chicago, Illinois, on June 6 2026. (Photo by KAMIL KRZACZYNSKI / AFP via Getty Images)
(KAMIL KRZACZYNSKI via Getty Images)
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 6:30 PM EDT
Sean Leahy
Curaçao opens up on Sunday against Germany and will then play Ecuador and Ivory Coast.
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 6:05 PM EDT
Sean Leahy
A look back at what the U.S. men’s national team has worn at previous World Cups and what they’ll wear on the pitch this summer.
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 5:48 PM EDT
Sean Leahy
The U.S. will send a delegation featuring Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Secretary of Transportation Sean Duffy and Secretary of Homeland Security Markwayne Mullin to SoFi Stadium for the USMNT’s opening match on Friday night.
There are no plans as of now for President Trump to attend, according to The Athletic. He was in New York City for Game 3 of the NBA Finals on Monday night. He’s expected to be in Washington D.C. this weekend for the UFC event that is set to take place at the White House.
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA - JUNE 09: General view of the Adidas Trionda, official match ball of the FIFA World Cup 2026 at SoFi Stadium on June 09, 2026 in Los Angeles, California. (Photo by Matt McNulty - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images)
(Matt McNulty - FIFA via Getty Images)
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 5:45 PM EDT
Sean Leahy
The Marktweg in The Hague, Netherlands is ready for the World Cup as every inch of this block is covered in orange. The Dutch begin its World Cup campaign on Sunday against Japan before facing Sweden and Tunisia.
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 5:10 PM EDT
Yahoo Sports Staff
Fifa President Gianni Infantino used his launch press conference for the World Cup on Wednesday to defend ticket prices and urge people to “chill” after a Somali referee was denied entry to the US.
The 2026 World Cup will begin on Thursday, with the US, Canada and Mexico serving as co-hosts. Many issues have already plagued the tournament, with Iran’s presence previously in doubt – amid the nation’s ongoing conflict with the US – and referee Omar Artan sent home this week after the Trump administration alleged he had links to “suspected members of terror organisations”.
Read more here.
MEXICO CITY, MEXICO - JUNE 10: Gianni Infantino, President of FIFA, speaks to the media during a FIFA World Cup 2026 Opening Press Conference at Mexico City Stadium on June 10, 2026 in Mexico City, Mexico. (Photo by Carl Recine/Getty Images)
(Carl Recine via Getty Images)
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 5:00 PM EDT
Nick Bromberg
With 48 teams in the 2026 World Cup, there's more disparity across the four-team groups than we're typically used to. Now that there are 12 groups instead of eight, it's hard to find a Group of Death with four exceptionally strong teams.
However, that doesn't mean there aren't any intriguing games in the group stage. Here are 10 pivotal matchups you'll see in group play before 32 teams move on to the knockout rounds.
Read more here.
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 4:54 PM EDT
Sean Leahy
Hours before he will be in his seat at Madison Square Garden for Game 4 of the NBA Finals between the Knicks and Spurs, director Spike Lee was at the New York Red Bulls training facility for Brazil's Wednesday training session.
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 2:45 PM EDT
Steven Goff
IRVINE, Calif. — When Chris Richards felt an opponent’s foot crash into his left ankle last month, pain mixed with fear.
The World Cup was less than a month away, and for the U.S. national team’s most important defender, thoughts of missing soccer’s spectacle for the second consecutive quadrennial rushed into his mind.
Read more here.
CHICAGO, ILLINOIS - JUNE 06: Chris Richards of the United States reacts prior to the international friendly match between United States and Germany at Soldier Field on June 06, 2026 in Chicago, Illinois. (Photo by Omar Vega/USSF/Getty Images)
(Omar Vega/USSF via Getty Images)
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 2:00 PM EDT
Yahoo Sports Staff
The wait is almost over.
The 2026 World Cup will begin in earnest on Thursday with Mexico taking on South Africa in the opener and continue Friday with the U.S. and Paraguay before group play really starts cranking up on Saturday.
But before the biggest FIFA tournament ever kicks off, some predictions are in order.
Read more here.
PUEBLA, MEXICO - JUNE 08: Fans of Spain kiss a replica of the FIFA World Cup Trophy prior to the international friendly match between Peru and Spain at Estadio Cuauhtémoc on June 08, 2026 in Puebla, Mexico. (Photo by Agustin Cuevas/Getty Images)
(Agustin Cuevas via Getty Images)
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 12:00 PM EDT
Paul Carr
The 2026 World Cup will be the biggest ever, having expanded to 48 teams, a 50% increase from the previous tournament. With two weeks until kickoff, the anticipation is building as teams get ready to play in the United States, Canada and Mexico.
Realistically, no more than a dozen teams are expected to vie for the title, but with 8-of-12 third-place teams reaching the Round of 32, every nation will dream of advancing and maybe pulling an upset or two in the knockout stage.
Read more here.
LILLE, FRANCE - JUNE 08: Captain, Kylian Mbappe #10 of Team France reacts before the International Friendly match between France and Northern Ireland at Stade Pierre Mauroy on June 08, 2026 in Lille, France. (Photo by Xavier Laine/Getty Images)
(Xavier Laine via Getty Images)
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 11:32 AM EDT
Sean Leahy
Mexico and South Africa will play the first match of the 2026 World Cup at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City on Thursday afternoon (3 p.m. ET, Fox). Students and government workers will be able to watch as Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum issued a decree to help ease traffic around the capital city.
There will be no school on Thursday for kids and government workers have been told to work from home.
"This decree is so that the opening ceremony can take place without traffic or problems on World Cup day," president Sheinbaum said.
Wed, June 10, 2026 at 11:20 AM EDT
Sean Leahy
How to watch the 2026 World Cup in the U.S.
Dates: June 11-July 19
Channels: Fox, FS1, Telemundo, Universo
Streaming: Fox One, Peacock, DirecTV, Fubo and more
